steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank follows the singular subject "it" and the adverb "regularly," and precedes the direct object "all its needles." It must be a finite main verb in the simple present that agrees with the singular subject "it." A third-person singular present verb takes an —s ending.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'shed': base form — lacks the —s ending required for a singular subject in simple present. ✗
  • Choice B — 'have shed': present perfect with plural auxiliary "have" — does not agree with singular "it." ✗
  • Choice C — 'sheds': simple present with —s ending — agrees with singular "it" and functions as the finite main verb. ✓
  • Choice D — 'shedding': present participle — cannot serve as a finite main verb without an auxiliary. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
